input.prompt:
I'm planning to add a new "Stepper" compound component to a shadcn/ui component library. I need to understand how existing compound components are structured in this codebase.

Please explore and read the source files for compound components that use React context to share state between sub-components. Good candidates to look at are:
- Accordion component (accordion.tsx)
- Tabs component (tabs.tsx)
- Dialog component (dialog.tsx)
- Any other compound component that has sub-components like Item, Trigger, Content, etc.

For each compound component found:
1. Show me the full file content
2. Note the pattern for context creation and usage
3. Note how sub-components are exported (named exports vs dot notation like Accordion.Root)
4. Note the TypeScript patterns used
5. Note any CVA (class-variance-authority) usage patterns

Also look for any existing Stepper component to make sure we won't duplicate it.

I'm planning to add a new "Stepper" compound component to a shadcn/ui component library. I need to understand how demo examples are created.

1. Find all demo example files (likely in a demos/ or examples/ folder, or files ending in -demo.tsx or similar)

6. Check if there are any utility files used by components (cn utility, etc.)

Show me actual file contents, especially for demo examples.

86	export const designSystemConfigSchema = z
87	  .object({
88	    base: z.enum(BASES.map((b) => b.name) as [BaseName, ...BaseName[]]),
89	    style: z.enum(STYLES.map((s) => s.name) as [StyleName, ...StyleName[]]),
90	    iconLibrary: z.enum(
91	      Object.keys(iconLibraries) as [IconLibraryName, ...IconLibraryName[]]
92	    ),
93	    baseColor: z
94	      .enum(
95	        BASE_COLORS.map((c) => c.name) as [BaseColorName, ...BaseColorName[]]
96	      )
97	      .default("neutral"),
98	    theme: z.enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ThemeName, ...ThemeName[]]),
99	    chartColor: z
100	      .enum(THEMES.map((t) => t.name) as [ChartColorName, ...ChartColorName[]])
101	      .default("neutral"),
102	    font: z.enum(fontValues).default("inter"),
103	    fontHeading: z.enum(fontHeadingValues).default("inherit"),
104	    item: z.string().optional(),
105	    rtl: z.boolean().default(false),
106	    menuAccent: z
107	      .enum(
108	        MENU_ACCENTS.map((a) => a.value) as [
109	          MenuAccentValue,
110	          ...MenuAccentValue[],
111	        ]
112	      )
113	      .default("subtle"),
114	    menuColor: z
115	      .enum(
116	        MENU_COLORS.map((m) => m.value) as [MenuColorValue, ...MenuColorValue[]]
117	      )
118	      .default("default"),
119	    radius: z
120	      .enum(RADII.map((r) => r.name) as [RadiusValue, ...RadiusValue[]])
121	      .default("default"),
122	    template: z
123	      .enum([
124	        "next",
125	        "next-monorepo",
126	        "start",
127	        "react-router",
128	        "vite",
129	        "vite-monorepo",
130	        "react-router-monorepo",
131	        "start-monorepo",
132	        "astro",
133	        "astro-monorepo",
134	        "laravel",
135	      ])
136	      .default("next")
137	      .optional(),
138	  })
139	  .refine(
140	    (data) => {
141	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
142	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.theme)
143	    },
144	    (data) => ({
145	      message: `Theme "${data.theme}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
146	      path: ["theme"],
147	    })
148	  )
149	  .refine(
150	    (data) => {
151	      const availableThemes = getThemesForBaseColor(data.baseColor)
152	      return availableThemes.some((t) => t.name === data.chartColor)
153	    },
154	    (data) => ({
155	      message: `Chart color "${data.chartColor}" is not available for base color "${data.baseColor}"`,
156	      path: ["chartColor"],
157	    })
158	  )

407	export function getThemesForBaseColor(baseColorName: string) {
408	  const baseColorNames = BASE_COLORS.map((bc) => bc.name)
409	
410	  return THEMES.filter((theme) => {
411	    if (theme.name === baseColorName) {
412	      return true
413	    }
414	    return !baseColorNames.includes(theme.name)
415	  })
416	}
417	
418	export function getBase(name: BaseName) {
419	  return BASES.find((base) => base.name === name)
420	}
421	
422	export function getStyle(name: StyleName) {
423	  return STYLES.find((style) => style.name === name)
424	}
425	
426	export function getTheme(name: ThemeName) {
427	  return THEMES.find((theme) => theme.name === name)
428	}
429	
430	export function getBaseColor(name: BaseColorName) {
431	  return BASE_COLORS.find((color) => color.name === name)
432	}
433	
434	export function getIconLibrary(name: IconLibraryName) {
435	  return iconLibraries[name]
436	}

438	// Builds a registry:theme item from a design system config.
439	export function buildRegistryTheme(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
440	  const baseColor = getBaseColor(config.baseColor)
441	  const theme = getTheme(config.theme)
442	
443	  if (!baseColor || !theme) {
444	    throw new Error(
445	      `Base color "${config.baseColor}" or theme "${config.theme}" not found`
446	    )
447	  }
448	
449	  // Merge base color and theme CSS vars.
450	  const lightVars: Record<string, string> = {
451	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
452	    ...(theme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
453	  }
454	  const darkVars: Record<string, string> = {
455	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
456	    ...(theme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
457	  }
458	  const themeVars: Record<string, string> = {}
459	
460	  // Apply chart color override.
461	  const chartTheme = getTheme(config.chartColor)
462	  if (chartTheme) {
463	    const chartLight = chartTheme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>
464	    const chartDark = chartTheme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>
465	    for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
466	      const key = `chart-${i}`
467	      if (chartLight?.[key]) lightVars[key] = chartLight[key]
468	      if (chartDark?.[key]) darkVars[key] = chartDark[key]
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  // Apply menu accent transformation.
473	  if (config.menuAccent === "bold") {
474	    lightVars.accent = lightVars.primary
475	    lightVars["accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
476	    darkVars.accent = darkVars.primary
477	    darkVars["accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
478	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent"] = lightVars.primary
479	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
480	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent"] = darkVars.primary
481	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
482	  }
483	
484	  // Apply radius transformation.
485	  if (config.radius && config.radius !== "default") {
486	    const radius = RADII.find((r) => r.name === config.radius)
487	    if (radius && radius.value) {
488	      lightVars.radius = radius.value
489	    }
490	  }
491	
492	  return {
493	    name: `${config.baseColor}-${config.theme}`,
494	    type: "registry:theme" as const,
495	    cssVars: {
496	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
497	      light: lightVars,
498	      dark: darkVars,
499	    },
500	  }
501	}
502	
503	// Builds a registry:base item from a design system config.
504	export function buildRegistryBase(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
505	  const baseItem = getBase(config.base)
506	  const iconLibraryItem = getIconLibrary(config.iconLibrary)
507	  const normalizedFontHeading =
508	    config.fontHeading === config.font ? "inherit" : config.fontHeading
509	
510	  if (!baseItem || !iconLibraryItem) {
511	    throw new Error(
512	      `Base "${config.base}" or icon library "${config.iconLibrary}" not found`
513	    )
514	  }
515	
516	  const registryTheme = buildRegistryTheme(config)
517	
518	  // Build dependencies.
519	  const dependencies = [
520	    `shadcn@${SHADCN_VERSION}`,
521	    "class-variance-authority",
522	    "tw-animate-css",
523	    ...(baseItem.dependencies ?? []),
524	    ...iconLibraryItem.packages,
525	  ]
526	
527	  const registryDependencies = ["utils"]
528	  const themeVars = {
529	    ...(registryTheme.cssVars?.theme ?? {}),
530	    ...(normalizedFontHeading === "inherit"
531	      ? { "--font-heading": getInheritedHeadingFontValue(config.font) }
532	      : {}),
533	  }
534	
535	  if (config.font) {
536	    registryDependencies.push(`font-${config.font}`)
537	  }
538	
539	  if (normalizedFontHeading !== "inherit") {
540	    registryDependencies.push(`font-heading-${normalizedFontHeading}`)
541	  }
542	
543	  return {
544	    name: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
545	    extends: "none",
546	    type: "registry:base" as const,
547	    config: {
548	      style: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
549	      iconLibrary: iconLibraryItem.name,
550	      rtl: config.rtl,
551	      menuColor: config.menuColor,
552	      menuAccent: config.menuAccent,
553	      tailwind: {
554	        baseColor: config.baseColor,
555	      },
556	    },
557	    dependencies,
558	    registryDependencies,
559	    cssVars: {
560	      ...registryTheme.cssVars,
561	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
562	    },
563	    css: {
564	      '@import "tw-animate-css"': {},
565	      '@import "shadcn/tailwind.css"': {},
566	      "@layer base": {
567	        "*": { "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {} },
568	        body: { "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {} },
569	      },
570	    },
571	    ...(config.rtl && {
572	      docs: `To learn how to set up the RTL provider and fonts for your app, see https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/rtl/${config.template === "next-monorepo" ? "next" : (config.template ?? "next")}`,
573	    }),
574	  }
575	}

24	/*
25	 * build-registry.mts is the single v4 registry pipeline.
26	 *
27	 * Source of truth:
28	 * - Authored raw component/registry source lives in registry/bases/base and
29	 *   registry/bases/radix.
30	 * - Authored demo source lives in examples/base and examples/radix.
31	 * - Style tokens live in registry/styles/style-*.css.
32	 *
33	 * Persistent outputs:
34	 * - registry/bases/__index__.tsx
35	 * - registry/__index__.tsx
36	 * - examples/__index__.tsx
37	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui/*
38	 * - styles/<base-style>/ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
39	 * - public/r/*
40	 *
41	 * Temporary outputs:
42	 * - registry/<base-style>/*
43	 * - registry-<style>.json
44	 *
45	 * Execution order:
46	 * 1. Build registry/bases/__index__.tsx from the authored base registries.
47	 * 2. Build temporary styled registries under registry/<base-style>.
48	 * 3. Build registry/__index__.tsx for runtime lookup across legacy styles and
49	 *    generated base-style combinations.
50	 * 4. Build examples/__index__.tsx from authored demos.
51	 * 5. Export public/r/* for every style through the shadcn CLI.
52	 * 6. Copy compiled ui/* from the temporary registries into styles/<style>/ui.
53	 * 7. Build styles/<style>/ui-rtl for base-nova and radix-nova only.
54	 * 8. Format the generated persistent outputs.
55	 * 9. Clean up the temporary registry/<base-style> trees and registry-*.json.
56	 */
57

41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

3	// Note: if you edit the schema here, you must also edit the schema in the

6	export const registryConfigItemSchema = z.union([
7	  // Simple string format: "https://example.com/{name}.json"
8	  z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
9	    message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
10	  }),
11	  // Advanced object format with auth options
12	  z.object({
13	    url: z.string().refine((s) => s.includes("{name}"), {
14	      message: "Registry URL must include {name} placeholder",
15	    }),
16	    params: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
17	    headers: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
18	  }),
19	])
20	
21	export const registryConfigSchema = z.record(
22	  z.string().refine((key) => key.startsWith("@"), {
23	    message: "Registry names must start with @ (e.g., @v0, @acme)",
24	  }),
25	  registryConfigItemSchema
26	)
27	
28	export const rawConfigSchema = z
29	  .object({
30	    $schema: z.string().optional(),
31	    style: z.string(),
32	    rsc: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
33	    tsx: z.coerce.boolean().default(true),
34	    tailwind: z.object({
35	      config: z.string().optional(),
36	      css: z.string(),
37	      baseColor: z.string(),
38	      cssVariables: z.boolean().default(true),
39	      prefix: z.string().default("").optional(),
40	    }),
41	    iconLibrary: z.string().optional(),
42	    rtl: z.coerce.boolean().default(false).optional(),
43	    menuColor: z
44	      .enum([
45	        "default",
46	        "inverted",
47	        "default-translucent",
48	        "inverted-translucent",
49	      ])
50	      .default("default")
51	      .optional(),
52	    menuAccent: z.enum(["subtle", "bold"]).default("subtle").optional(),
53	    aliases: z.object({
54	      components: z.string(),
55	      utils: z.string(),
56	      ui: z.string().optional(),
57	      lib: z.string().optional(),
58	      hooks: z.string().optional(),
59	    }),
60	    registries: registryConfigSchema.optional(),
61	  })
62	  .strict()
63	
64	export const configSchema = rawConfigSchema.extend({
65	  resolvedPaths: z.object({
66	    cwd: z.string(),
67	    tailwindConfig: z.string(),
68	    tailwindCss: z.string(),

70	    components: z.string(),
71	    lib: z.string(),
72	    hooks: z.string(),
73	    ui: z.string(),
74	  }),
75	})
76	
77	// TODO: type the key.
78	// Okay for now since I don't want a breaking change.

80	
81	export const registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
82	  "registry:lib",
83	  "registry:block",
84	  "registry:component",
85	  "registry:ui",
86	  "registry:hook",
87	  "registry:page",
88	  "registry:file",
89	  "registry:theme",
90	  "registry:style",
91	  "registry:item",
92	  "registry:base",
93	  "registry:font",
94	
95	  // Internal use only.
96	  "registry:example",
97	  "registry:internal",
98	])
99	
100	export const registryItemFileSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
101	  // Target is required for registry:file and registry:page
102	  z.object({
103	    path: z.string(),
104	    content: z.string().optional(),
105	    type: z.enum(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
106	    target: z.string(),
107	  }),
108	  z.object({
109	    path: z.string(),
110	    content: z.string().optional(),
111	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:file", "registry:page"]),
112	    target: z.string().optional(),
113	  }),
114	])
115	
116	export const registryItemTailwindSchema = z.object({
117	  config: z
118	    .object({
119	      content: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
120	      theme: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
121	      plugins: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
122	    })
123	    .optional(),
124	})
125	
126	export const registryItemCssVarsSchema = z.object({
127	  theme: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),
138	  ])
139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),

150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
180	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
181	    type: z.literal("registry:base"),
182	    config: rawConfigSchema.deepPartial().optional(),
183	  }),
184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
185	    type: z.literal("registry:font"),
186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])

193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>

41	function Button({
42	  className,
43	  variant = "default",
44	  size = "default",
45	  asChild = false,
46	  ...props
47	}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
48	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
49	    asChild?: boolean
50	  }) {
51	  const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "button"
52	
53	  return (
54	    <Comp
55	      data-slot="button"
56	      data-variant={variant}
57	      data-size={size}
58	      className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))}
59	      {...props}
60	    />
61	  )
62	}
63	
64	export { Button, buttonVariants }

I'm planning to add a new "Stepper" compound component to a shadcn/ui component library. I need to understand how existing compound components are structured in this codebase.

Please explore and read the source files for compound components that use React context to share state between sub-components. Good candidates to look at are:
- Accordion component (accordion.tsx)
- Tabs component (tabs.tsx)
- Dialog component (dialog.tsx)
- Any other compound component that has sub-components like Item, Trigger, Content, etc.

For each compound component found:
1. Show me the full file content
2. Note the pattern for context creation and usage
3. Note how sub-components are exported (named exports vs dot notation like Accordion.Root)
4. Note the TypeScript patterns used
5. Note any CVA (class-variance-authority) usage patterns

Also look for any existing Stepper component to make sure we won't duplicate it.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report for the user. ## Project Structure & Registry System Analysis I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library and have a complete understanding of the structure and registry system. Here's your comprehensive guide: --- ### 1. **Root Directory Structure** ``` / ├── apps/ │ └── v4/ # Main v4 application (Next.js) │ ├── components/ # UI components │ ├── registry/ # Registry source and build output │ ├── registry/new-york-v4/ # v4 style components │ │ ├── ui/ # UI components │ │ ├── examples/ # Component demos/examples │ │ ├── blocks/ # Template blocks │ │ ├── charts/ # Chart components │ │ ├── hooks/ # Custom hooks │ │ ├── lib/ # Utilities │ │ └── internal/ # Internal components │ ├── registry.json # Generated master registry (outputs all items) │ └── scripts/ # Build scripts │ ├── packages/ │ ├── shadcn/ # Core shadcn package │ │ └── src/ │ │ └── registry/ │ │ └── schema.ts # Registry schema definitions │ └── tests/ │ ├── templates/ # Project templates └── skills/ # CLI skills ``` --- ### 2. **Registry Schema & Types** The core registry schema is defined in `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`: **Main Registry Item Fields:** ```typescript { name: string // Unique identifier type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example" | "registry:block" | etc. title?: string // Display title description?: string // Description author?: string // Component author // Dependencies dependencies?: string[] // NPM packages needed devDependencies?: string[] // Dev-only packages registryDependencies?: string[] // Other registry items required // Files files: Array<{ path: string // File location (relative) type: string // File type (registry:ui, registry:example, etc.) target?: string // Target path for installation content?: string // Optional inline content }> // Styling & Config tailwind?: { config?: { content?: string[] theme?: Record plugins?: string[] } } cssVars?: { theme?: Record // Theme variables light?: Record // Light mode colors dark?: Record // Dark mode colors } css?: Record // CSS-in-JS styles // Metadata meta?: Record // Custom metadata docs?: string // Documentation link categories?: string[] // Tags/categories } ``` **Special Types:** - `registry:base` - Has a `config` field for design system settings - `registry:font` - Has a `font` field with provider/import info - `registry:ui` - Basic UI component - `registry:example` - Component demo/usage example - `registry:block` - Full template/page block --- ### 3. **Component Registration Pattern** All components are registered in the `_registry.ts` files. PROJECT STRUCTURE **Root Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474213361-67thyp` **Key Directories:** - `/apps/v4/` - Main Next.js application for the component library website - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/` - Contains all UI components and examples organized by theme - `ui/` - Component source files (pure React components) - `examples/` - Demo/example files showcasing component usage ### 2. DEMO EXAMPLES ORGANIZATION **Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` The examples folder contains 200+ demo files with naming patterns like: - `{component-name}-demo.tsx` (basic demo) - `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx` (variant demonstrations) - Examples: `button-demo.tsx`, `accordion-demo.tsx`, `carousel-api.tsx`, `form-rhf-demo.tsx` **Registry Definition:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` Each demo is registered in the `_registry.ts` file with metadata including: - Component name identifier - Type: `registry:example` - Dependencies: which UI components it uses (`registryDependencies`) - File references ### 3. ACTUAL DEMO FILE CONTENTS #### Example 1: Button Demo (Simple Component) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/button-demo.tsx` ```typescript import { ArrowUpIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" export default function ButtonDemo() { return (
) } ``` **Key Patterns:** - Default export function with demo name (`ButtonDemo`) - Imports from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/{component}` - Uses Tailwind classes for layout - Simple, focused examples #### Example 2: Accordion Demo (Compound Component) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/accordion-demo.tsx` ```typescript import { Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion" export default function AccordionDemo() { return ( Product Information {/* content */} {/* more items */} ) } ``` **Key Patterns:** - Imports multiple sub-components - Shows composition and nesting - Demonstrates props like `type="single"`, `collapsible`, `defaultValue` - Complete, realistic usage example #### Example 3: Form with React Hook Form (Advanced) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/form-rhf-demo.tsx` ```typescript "use client" import * as React from "react" import { zodResolver } from "@hookform/resolvers/zod" import { Controller, useForm } from "react-hook-form" import { toast } from "sonner" import * as z from "zod" import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card" import { Field, FieldDescription, FieldError, FieldGroup, FieldLabel } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/field" import { Input } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input" import { InputGroup, InputGroupAddon, InputGroupText, InputGroupTextarea } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input-group" const formSchema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(5, "Bug title must be at least 5 characters."), description: z.string().min(20, "Description must be at least 20 characters."), }) export default function BugReportForm() { const form = useForm>({ resolver: zodResolver(formSchema), defaultValues: { title: "", description: "" }, }) function onSubmit(data: z.infer) { toast("You submitted the following values:", { // toast config }) } return ( {/* form content */} ) } ``` **Key Patterns:** - Client component (`"use client"`) - Uses external libraries (react-hook-form, zod) - Shows validation, form state management - Real-world complexity #### Example 4: Carousel Demo (With Hooks) **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/carousel-demo.tsx` ```typescript import * as React from "react" import { Card, CardContent } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card" import { Carousel, CarouselContent, CarouselItem, CarouselNext, CarouselPrevious, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel" export default function CarouselDemo() { return ( {Array.from({ length: 5 }).map((_, index) => (
{index + 1}
))} ) } ``` **Key Patterns:** - Shows dynamic rendering with array mapping - Demonstrates compound component composition - Responsive layout ### 4. TSCONFIG & ALIAS CONFIGURATION **File:** `/apps/v4/tsconfig.json` ```json { "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": ".", "paths": { "@/*": ["./*"], "react": ["./node_modules/@types/react"] }, "jsx": "react-jsx", "moduleResolution": "bundler" } } ``` **Key Aliases:** - `@/*` resolves to the app root (base path: `.`) - Examples use: `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button` - Examples use: `@/lib/utils` for utilities ### 5. Slot.Root : "button" return ( ) } export { Button, buttonVariants } ``` ### 7. COMPOUND COMPONENT EXAMPLE: CAROUSEL **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/carousel.tsx` (simplified) Uses React Context pattern: ```typescript "use client" import * as React from "react" import useEmblaCarousel from "embla-carousel-react" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" type CarouselContextProps = { carouselRef: ReturnType[0] api: ReturnType[1] scrollPrev: () => void scrollNext: () => void canScrollPrev: boolean canScrollNext: boolean } const CarouselContext = React.createContext(null) function useCarousel() { const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a ") return context } function Carousel({ orientation = "horizontal", ...props }) { const [carouselRef, api] = useEmblaCarousel(...) // state management for scroll state return ( {/* carousel content */} ) } function CarouselContent() { const { carouselRef, orientation } = useCarousel() return
...
} function CarouselItem() { const { orientation } = useCarousel() return
...
} export { Carousel, CarouselContent, CarouselItem, CarouselPrevious, CarouselNext } ``` ### 8. UTILITY FUNCTION **File:** `/apps/v4/lib/utils.ts` ```typescript import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx" import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge" export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)) } ``` Used in every component to merge Tailwind classes intelligently (clsx + tailwind-merge). ### 9. COMPONENT REGISTRY DEFINITION **File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (excerpt) ```typescript export const ui: Registry["items"] = [ { name: "button", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["radix-ui"], files: [{ path: "ui/button.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], }, { name: "carousel", type: "registry:ui", files: [{ path: "ui/carousel.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], registryDependencies: ["button"], dependencies: ["embla-carousel-react"], }, ] ``` ### 10. 5	import { ToggleGroup as ToggleGroupPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
6	
7	import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
8	import { toggleVariants } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/toggle"
9	
10	const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12	    spacing?: number
13	  }
14	>({
15	  size: "default",
16	  variant: "default",
17	  spacing: 0,
18	})
19	
20	function ToggleGroup({
21	  className,
22	  variant,
23	  size,
24	  spacing = 0,
25	  children,
26	  ...props
27	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
28	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
29	    spacing?: number
30	  }) {
31	  return (
32	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
33	      data-slot="toggle-group"
34	      data-variant={variant}
35	      data-size={size}
36	      data-spacing={spacing}
37	      style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
38	      className={cn(
39	        "group/toggle-group flex w-fit items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-md data-[spacing=default]:data-[variant=outline]:shadow-xs",
40	        className
41	      )}
42	      {...props}
43	    >
44	      <ToggleGroupContext.Provider value={{ variant, size, spacing }}>
45	        {children}
46	      </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
47	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	function ToggleGroupItem({
52	  className,
53	  children,
54	  variant,
55	  size,
56	  ...props
57	}: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
58	  VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
59	  const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
60	
61	  return (
62	    <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
63	      data-slot="toggle-group-item"
64	      data-variant={context.variant || variant}
65	      data-size={context.size || size}
66	      data-spacing={context.spacing}
67	      className={cn(
68	        toggleVariants({
69	          variant: context.variant || variant,
70	          size: context.size || size,
71	        }),
72	        "w-auto min-w-0 shrink-0 px-3 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10",
73	        "data-[spacing=0]:rounded-none data-[spacing=0]:shadow-none data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-l-md data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-r-md data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l",
74	        className
75	      )}
76	      {...props}
77	    >
78	      {children}
79	    </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
80	  )
81	}
82	
83	export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }

For state management, the parent Stepper tracks the active step while each StepperItem determines its own state based on position—steps before active are completed, the active step is active, and later steps are pending. The cleanest pattern would be passing step values as an array to the Stepper component, letting each item look itself up to determine if it's completed or pending. For the API design, I'm leaning toward a string-based `value` system similar to how Tabs works—this avoids requiring explicit step numbers while still supporting clickable triggers that update the active step. Tabs wraps Radix UI which manages state internally, but since we're building a custom Stepper without Radix primitives, I need to handle state management myself. Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me design the complete implementation plan. --- ## Implementation Plan: Stepper Compound Component ### Overview The Stepper is a fully custom compound component (no Radix UI primitive exists for this pattern). It follows the carousel/toggle-group context patterns and the field.tsx CVA+orientation pattern. It uses two nested contexts: one for the root stepper and one per step item. --- ## 1. File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Structure and Architecture **Two context layers:** - `StepperContext` — owned by `Stepper`, holds `{ value, onValueChange, orientation }` - `StepperItemContext` — owned by `StepperItem`, holds `{ value, disabled, completed }` **CVA usage:** - `stepperIndicatorVariants` for the circular badge: driven by `data-state` via Tailwind attribute selectors, not a CVA variant parameter (so external override via className is easy and state is encoded via `data-state` on the element per library convention) - `stepperSeparatorVariants` for horizontal vs vertical line **Derived step state logic** (inside `StepperItem`): Computes `data-state` as `"disabled" | "active" | "completed" | "pending"` by checking `disabled` prop first, then comparing item `value` to root `value`, then `completed` prop. ### Component-by-Component Plan #### Type Definitions ``` type StepperContextProps = { value: string onValueChange: (value: string) => void orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" } type StepperItemContextProps = { value: string disabled: boolean completed: boolean } ``` #### `StepperContext` and `useStepper` - `React.createContext(null)` - `useStepper()` throws if called outside `` - Exported as a named export #### `StepperItemContext` (internal, not exported) - `React.createContext(null)` - Internal hook `useStepperItem()` for child components to consume #### `Stepper` (root) - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { value: string; onValueChange: (value: string) => void; orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" }` - `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation={orientation}` - CSS: `"flex"` + `data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-row data-[orientation=horizontal]:items-center` + `data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col` - Full class string: `"flex data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-row data-[orientation=horizontal]:items-center data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col"` - Wraps children in `StepperContext.Provider` #### `StepperItem` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { value: string; disabled?: boolean; completed?: boolean }` - Computes `state`: ``` const state = disabled ? "disabled" : stepperValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" ``` where `stepperValue` comes from `useStepper().value` - `data-slot="stepper-item"`, `data-state={state}`, `data-disabled={disabled}` - CSS for horizontal: `"flex flex-col items-center"` is not quite right — in horizontal mode the item itself typically takes `"flex items-center"` and is part of the flex row at the root level. But with separator as a sibling in horizontal mode, the item itself is `"flex flex-col items-center gap-2"` — the indicator + title stacked vertically within the horizontal flow. - Actually: in horizontal mode `StepperItem` holds `StepperTrigger` (which is `flex-row items-center gap-2` for indicator+title side by side or stacked). In vertical mode it holds `StepperTrigger` + `StepperSeparator` + `StepperContent` stacked. - CSS on item: `"group/stepper-item relative flex data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col"` — orientation comes via CSS parent context selector Better approach: read orientation via `useStepper()` and apply classes conditionally: ``` orientation === "horizontal" ? "flex flex-col items-center gap-1.5" : "flex flex-col" ``` - Wraps in `StepperItemContext.Provider` with `{ value, disabled: !!disabled, completed: !!completed }` #### `StepperTrigger` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"button">` - Calls `onValueChange(itemValue)` on click if not disabled - `data-slot="stepper-trigger"`, `data-state={state}` forwarded from item context - Uses `disabled` attribute from item context - CSS: `"inline-flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer rounded-sm outline-none focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:border-ring disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"` - In horizontal mode: `StepperTrigger` wraps indicator + title stacked or side-by-side — the trigger is a flex container. The required usage from the spec has `StepperIndicator` + `StepperTitle` as children, so the trigger is `"inline-flex flex-col items-center gap-1"` for horizontal, and `"inline-flex flex-row items-center gap-2"` for vertical. - Use orientation from context: `orientation === "horizontal" ? "flex-col items-center" : "flex-row items-center"` - Full class: `cn("inline-flex gap-2 cursor-pointer rounded-sm outline-none focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:border-ring disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50", orientation === "horizontal" ? "flex-col items-center" : "flex-row items-center", className)` #### `StepperIndicator` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"span">` - `data-slot="stepper-indicator"`, `data-state={state}` from item context - Renders as a `` circle showing step number or icon slot - State-driven styling using `data-state` Tailwind selectors: - `data-[state=active]`: `bg-primary text-primary-foreground` - `data-[state=completed]`: `bg-primary text-primary-foreground` - `data-[state=pending]`: `border-2 border-muted-foreground/30 text-muted-foreground bg-background` - `data-[state=disabled]`: `border-2 border-muted-foreground/20 text-muted-foreground/50 bg-background` - Base CSS: `"inline-flex size-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-xs font-medium transition-colors"` - Full class: ``` "inline-flex size-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-xs font-medium transition-colors" "data-[state=active]:bg-primary data-[state=active]:text-primary-foreground" "data-[state=completed]:bg-primary data-[state=completed]:text-primary-foreground" "data-[state=pending]:border-2 data-[state=pending]:border-muted-foreground/30 data-[state=pending]:text-muted-foreground data-[state=pending]:bg-background" "data-[state=disabled]:border-2 data-[state=disabled]:border-muted-foreground/20 data-[state=disabled]:text-muted-foreground/50 data-[state=disabled]:bg-background" ``` #### `StepperTitle` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"p">` - `data-slot="stepper-title"`, `data-state={state}` - CSS: `"text-sm font-medium leading-snug data-[state=active]:text-foreground data-[state=pending]:text-muted-foreground data-[state=disabled]:text-muted-foreground/50 data-[state=completed]:text-foreground"` #### `StepperDescription` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"p">` - `data-slot="stepper-description"`, `data-state={state}` - CSS: `"text-xs text-muted-foreground leading-normal data-[state=disabled]:opacity-50"` #### `StepperSeparator` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` - Reads `orientation` from `StepperContext` via `useStepper()` - `data-slot="stepper-separator"`, `data-orientation={orientation}` - In horizontal mode: a horizontal line between items, should grow to fill space — used as a sibling of `StepperItem` in the root flex row. CSS: `"h-px flex-1 bg-border data-[orientation=horizontal]:block data-[orientation=vertical]:hidden"` — but wait, in vertical mode the separator is placed _inside_ `StepperItem` to be a vertical connector. - Correct approach: the separator always renders a line, and its shape adapts: - Horizontal mode (sibling between items): `"h-px min-w-4 flex-1 shrink-0 bg-border"` - Vertical mode (child inside `StepperItem`, below trigger): `"ms-4 mt-1 mb-1 w-px self-start bg-border"` — where `ms-4` aligns it under the center of the 8-unit indicator, and it grows vertically. Actually for the vertical connector, `"ml-[calc(theme(size.4)-0.5px)] mt-0.5 mb-0.5 w-px min-h-4 flex-1 self-stretch bg-border"` aligns it with the indicator center. - Unified approach using `data-orientation`: ``` "shrink-0 bg-border" "data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-1 data-[orientation=horizontal]:min-w-4" "data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px data-[orientation=vertical]:min-h-4 data-[orientation=vertical]:self-stretch data-[orientation=vertical]:ms-[15px]" ``` The `ms-[15px]` (15px = half of 32px indicator) aligns the vertical bar under indicator center. #### `StepperContent` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` - Reads `value` from `StepperItemContext` and `value` from `StepperContext` - Only renders children when step is active: `if (stepperValue !== itemValue) return null` - `data-slot="stepper-content"`, `data-state={state}` - CSS: `"ms-10 pb-4 text-sm"` — `ms-10` aligns content under the indicator in vertical mode (32px indicator + 8px gap = 40px = ms-10) #### `useStepper` export - Simply re-exports `useCarousel`-style hook: `export { useStepper }` ### Named Exports ``` export { Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperIndicator, StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent, useStepper, } ``` --- ## 2. File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` This is the **horizontal demo**. It uses `"use client"` and local state to control the active step. ### Pattern ``` "use client" import * as React from "react" import { CheckIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Stepper, StepperContent, StepperDescription, StepperIndicator, StepperItem, StepperSeparator, StepperTitle, StepperTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" export default function StepperDemo() { const [step, setStep] = React.useState("account") const steps = [ { value: "account", label: "Account", description: "Create your account" }, { value: "profile", label: "Profile", description: "Set up your profile" }, { value: "review", label: "Review", description: "Confirm your details" }, ] return (
{steps.map((s, index) => ( x.value === step) > index} > {steps.findIndex(x => x.value === step) > index ? : index + 1} {s.label} {index < steps.length - 1 && } ))}
) } ``` Key decisions: - `"use client"` is required because of `React.useState` - Uses `React.Fragment` with key to interleave items and separators - `completed` prop computed from whether step index precedes current active index - `CheckIcon` from `lucide-react` is used inside `StepperIndicator` for completed steps --- ## 3. File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-vertical.tsx` This is the **vertical demo** with content panels and separator inside each item. ### Pattern ``` "use client" import * as React from "react" import { CheckIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" import { Stepper, StepperContent, StepperDescription, StepperIndicator, StepperItem, StepperSeparator, StepperTitle, StepperTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" const steps = [ { value: "step-1", title: "Create your account", description: "Enter your email and password", content: "Provide a valid email address and a secure password to get started.", }, { value: "step-2", title: "Verify your email", description: "Check your inbox", content: "We sent a verification link to your email. Click it to continue.", }, { value: "step-3", title: "Complete your profile", description: "Add your details", content: "Fill in your name, bio, and profile picture to personalize your account.", }, ] export default function StepperVertical() { const [step, setStep] = React.useState("step-1") const currentIndex = steps.findIndex(s => s.value === step) function handleNext() { const next = steps[currentIndex + 1] if (next) setStep(next.value) } function handleBack() { const prev = steps[currentIndex - 1] if (prev) setStep(prev.value) } return (
{steps.map((s, index) => ( index} > {currentIndex > index ? : index + 1}
{s.title} {s.description}
{index < steps.length - 1 && }

{s.content}

{currentIndex > 0 && ( )} {currentIndex < steps.length - 1 && ( )}
))}
) } ``` --- ## 4. Registry Entries ### `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` — Insertion Insert after the closing brace of the `spinner` entry (line 563, `},`) and before the `switch` entry (line 564). The new block to insert is: ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["class-variance-authority"], files: [ { path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, ``` **Exact insertion point**: After line 563 (` },`) which closes the `spinner` entry, before line 564 (` {` which opens `switch`). The new block sits at lines 564–572, pushing `switch` and everything after down by 9 lines. Note: `class-variance-authority` is listed as a dependency because CVA is used internally for separator/indicator variants. This matches the `spinner` entry which also lists it. Lucide icons used in demos are not listed as a component dependency (they are in the examples, not the UI component itself — the component itself renders children for the indicator slot). ### `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` — Insertion Insert after line 2124 (the `},` closing the `spinner-item` entry) and before line 2125 (`{` opening `switch-demo`). Two new entries: ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, { name: "stepper-vertical", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-vertical.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, ``` Note: `stepper-vertical` also lists `button` as a `registryDependency` because the demo uses ` ) } ``` Wait — there is an issue with the inline `React.useContext` calls inside the render function body of `StepperTrigger`. Hooks cannot be called conditionally. Let me re-think: The cleanest approach: `StepperTrigger` calls `useStepper()` and `useStepperItem()` directly. Both are always called unconditionally at the top. The `aria-current` check happens inline as a plain expression using already-stored variables. ```typescript function StepperTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"button">) { const { value: activeValue, onValueChange, orientation } = useStepper() const { value, disabled } = useStepperItem() return ( ) } ``` #### `StepperIndicator` ```typescript function StepperIndicator({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"span">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = disabled ? "disabled" : activeValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" return ( {children} ) } ``` Note that the `state` derivation is duplicated in both `StepperItem` and in `StepperIndicator` / `StepperTitle` / `StepperDescription`. This is intentional — each sub-component independently reads from both contexts to set its own `data-state`. This is cleaner than trying to pass computed state down through a third context value, and keeps each component self-contained. #### `StepperTitle` ```typescript function StepperTitle({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"p">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = disabled ? "disabled" : activeValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" return (

) } ``` #### `StepperDescription` ```typescript function StepperDescription({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"p">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = disabled ? "disabled" : activeValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" return (

) } ``` To reduce the state derivation duplication, a small helper at the module level is the right call: ```typescript function deriveStepState( activeValue: string, itemValue: string, disabled: boolean, completed: boolean ): "active" | "completed" | "pending" | "disabled" { if (disabled) return "disabled" if (activeValue === itemValue) return "active" if (completed) return "completed" return "pending" } ``` #### `StepperSeparator` ```typescript function StepperSeparator({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { const { orientation } = useStepper() return (

) } ``` #### `StepperContent` ```typescript function StepperContent({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = deriveStepState(activeValue, value, disabled, completed) if (state !== "active") { return null } return (
{children}
) } ``` --- ## 9. Step-by-Step Implementation Sequence 1. **Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`**: - Write contexts (`StepperContext`, `StepperItemContext`) - Write `useStepper` hook - Write `deriveStepState` helper - Write all 8 sub-components in order: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent` - Add named export block at the bottom 2. **Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`**: - Horizontal demo with 3 steps, local state, `CheckIcon` for completed steps 3. **Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-vertical.tsx`**: - Vertical demo with 3 steps, Back/Continue buttons, content panels 4. **Modify `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`**: - Insert 9-line `stepper` entry between the end of the `spinner` entry (after line 563) and the start of the `switch` entry (before what is currently line 564) 5. **Modify `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`**: - Insert `stepper-demo` and `stepper-vertical` entries between the end of the `spinner-item` entry (after line 2124) and the start of the `switch-demo` entry (before what is currently line 2125) --- ## 10. Potential Challenges **Challenge 1: `StepperSeparator` in vertical mode positioning** The `ms-[15px]` magic number aligns the 1px vertical line with the center of the 32px (`size-8`) indicator. If consumers use a different indicator size, this will be misaligned. Mitigation: document that `size-8` is assumed; provide a CSS variable override path via `className`. **Challenge 2: State duplication across sub-components** Each sub-component that needs state (`StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperContent`) recomputes it from the two contexts. The `deriveStepState` helper centralizes the logic so there is one source of truth for the algorithm, even if it is called in multiple places. **Challenge 3: Controlled-only pattern** The component is controlled-only (requires `value` + `onValueChange`). This matches the usage pattern specified and is simpler than supporting uncontrolled mode. Consumers must manage state themselves — the demos show this with `React.useState`. **Challenge 4: Separator orientation in vertical layout** `StepperSeparator` reads `orientation` from the root `StepperContext`, not from its positional context (sibling vs. child). In horizontal mode, separators are siblings of `StepperItem`. In vertical mode, they are children of `StepperItem`. The orientation context correctly drives the CSS in both cases. The consumer is responsible for placing the separator in the right structural position. **Challenge 5: `data-state` on `StepperItem` wrapper vs. trigger** Both `StepperItem` and `StepperTrigger` set `data-state`. This is intentional duplication — `StepperItem`'s `data-state` allows parent CSS selectors (`group-data-[state=active]/stepper-item:...`) on children. `StepperTrigger`'s `aria-current` handles the accessibility concern separately. --- ## 11. Verification Steps 1. **TypeScript compilation**: Run `tsc --noEmit` from `apps/v4/` — all components should have proper types, no implicit `any` 2. **Registry validation**: Run `npx shadcn build` or the project's registry build command to verify both `_registry.ts` entries parse correctly 3. **Horizontal layout test**: Render `stepper-demo.tsx` — verify steps are side-by-side, separator fills available width, indicator/title stack vertically within each item 4. **Vertical layout test**: Render `stepper-vertical.tsx` — verify separator is a vertical line aligned under indicator, content panels show/hide correctly 5. **State transitions**: Click each step trigger — verify `data-state` changes on `StepperItem`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle` update styles correctly 6. **Disabled step**: Add `disabled` to one item — verify trigger is `disabled`, styles reflect muted/opacity treatment 7. **Keyboard navigation**: Tab through triggers — verify focus rings appear correctly matching the ring pattern from `button.tsx` 8. **`aria-current="step"`**: Inspect active trigger in browser DevTools — verify attribute is present on active step, absent on others 9. **`aria-hidden`**: Verify `StepperIndicator` and `StepperSeparator` both have `aria-hidden="true"` in rendered output 10. **`useStepper` hook**: Call `useStepper()` outside a `` — verify error is thrown with the correct message --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474213361-67thyp/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` (new file to create) - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474213361-67thyp/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` (insert stepper entry after line 563) - tool_use_result.prompt: I need you to design the detailed implementation for a new "Stepper" compound component for a shadcn/ui component library. Do NOT write any code - only describe the plan. ## Context This is a Next.js shadcn/ui project at `apps/v4/`. Components live in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/`. Examples (demos) live in `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/`. Registry entries live in `_registry.ts` files in each folder. ## Key patterns observed from reading existing code: ### Component patterns: - `"use client"` at top for components with state - `import * as React from "react"` always - `import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"` for class merging - `import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"` for variants - `data-slot="component-name"` on every component's root element - `data-state="..."` for dynamic states - `data-orientation="..."` for orientation - Named exports only (no dot notation like `Stepper.Item`) - TypeScript: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` intersection for props ### Context pattern (from `carousel.tsx`): ```tsx const CarouselContext = React.createContext(null) function useCarousel() { const context = React.useContext(CarouselContext) if (!context) throw new Error("useCarousel must be used within a ") return context } // Root component wraps with Provider, sub-components call the hook ``` ### CVA + orientation (from `field.tsx`): ```tsx const fieldVariants = cva("group/field flex w-full gap-3", { variants: { orientation: { vertical: [...], horizontal: [...] }, }, defaultVariants: { orientation: "vertical" }, }) ``` ### UI registry entry (from `ui/_registry.ts`): ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["class-variance-authority"], files: [{ path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui" }], } ``` ### Example registry entry (from `examples/_registry.ts`): ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [{ path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example" }], } ``` ### Demo pattern (from `carousel-demo.tsx`, `accordion-demo.tsx`): ```tsx "use client" import * as React from "react" import { Component, ... } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component" export default function ComponentDemo() { ... } ``` ## Requirements Design a plan to implement a "Stepper" compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows. Must include these sub-components: 1. `Stepper` - Root container with React Context, supports `value` (controlled active step), `onValueChange`, `orientation` ("horizontal" | "vertical") 2. `StepperItem` - Individual step wrapper; props: `value` (string), `disabled?`, `completed?`; provides item context; sets `data-state` to "active"/"completed"/"pending"/"disabled" 3. `StepperTrigger` - Clickable `

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me design the complete implementation plan. --- ## Implementation Plan: Stepper Compound Component ### Overview The Stepper is a fully custom compound component (no Radix UI primitive exists for this pattern). It follows the carousel/toggle-group context patterns and the field.tsx CVA+orientation pattern. It uses two nested contexts: one for the root stepper and one per step item. --- ## 1. File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Structure and Architecture **Two context layers:** - `StepperContext` — owned by `Stepper`, holds `{ value, onValueChange, orientation }` - `StepperItemContext` — owned by `StepperItem`, holds `{ value, disabled, completed }` **CVA usage:** - `stepperIndicatorVariants` for the circular badge: driven by `data-state` via Tailwind attribute selectors, not a CVA variant parameter (so external override via className is easy and state is encoded via `data-state` on the element per library convention) - `stepperSeparatorVariants` for horizontal vs vertical line **Derived step state logic** (inside `StepperItem`): Computes `data-state` as `"disabled" | "active" | "completed" | "pending"` by checking `disabled` prop first, then comparing item `value` to root `value`, then `completed` prop. ### Component-by-Component Plan #### Type Definitions ``` type StepperContextProps = { value: string onValueChange: (value: string) => void orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" } type StepperItemContextProps = { value: string disabled: boolean completed: boolean } ``` #### `StepperContext` and `useStepper` - `React.createContext(null)` - `useStepper()` throws if called outside `` - Exported as a named export #### `StepperItemContext` (internal, not exported) - `React.createContext(null)` - Internal hook `useStepperItem()` for child components to consume #### `Stepper` (root) - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { value: string; onValueChange: (value: string) => void; orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical" }` - `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation={orientation}` - CSS: `"flex"` + `data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-row data-[orientation=horizontal]:items-center` + `data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col` - Full class string: `"flex data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-row data-[orientation=horizontal]:items-center data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col"` - Wraps children in `StepperContext.Provider` #### `StepperItem` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div"> & { value: string; disabled?: boolean; completed?: boolean }` - Computes `state`: ``` const state = disabled ? "disabled" : stepperValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" ``` where `stepperValue` comes from `useStepper().value` - `data-slot="stepper-item"`, `data-state={state}`, `data-disabled={disabled}` - CSS for horizontal: `"flex flex-col items-center"` is not quite right — in horizontal mode the item itself typically takes `"flex items-center"` and is part of the flex row at the root level. But with separator as a sibling in horizontal mode, the item itself is `"flex flex-col items-center gap-2"` — the indicator + title stacked vertically within the horizontal flow. - Actually: in horizontal mode `StepperItem` holds `StepperTrigger` (which is `flex-row items-center gap-2` for indicator+title side by side or stacked). In vertical mode it holds `StepperTrigger` + `StepperSeparator` + `StepperContent` stacked. - CSS on item: `"group/stepper-item relative flex data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-col data-[orientation=vertical]:flex-col"` — orientation comes via CSS parent context selector Better approach: read orientation via `useStepper()` and apply classes conditionally: ``` orientation === "horizontal" ? "flex flex-col items-center gap-1.5" : "flex flex-col" ``` - Wraps in `StepperItemContext.Provider` with `{ value, disabled: !!disabled, completed: !!completed }` #### `StepperTrigger` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"button">` - Calls `onValueChange(itemValue)` on click if not disabled - `data-slot="stepper-trigger"`, `data-state={state}` forwarded from item context - Uses `disabled` attribute from item context - CSS: `"inline-flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer rounded-sm outline-none focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:border-ring disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"` - In horizontal mode: `StepperTrigger` wraps indicator + title stacked or side-by-side — the trigger is a flex container. The required usage from the spec has `StepperIndicator` + `StepperTitle` as children, so the trigger is `"inline-flex flex-col items-center gap-1"` for horizontal, and `"inline-flex flex-row items-center gap-2"` for vertical. - Use orientation from context: `orientation === "horizontal" ? "flex-col items-center" : "flex-row items-center"` - Full class: `cn("inline-flex gap-2 cursor-pointer rounded-sm outline-none focus-visible:ring-[3px] focus-visible:ring-ring/50 focus-visible:border-ring disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50", orientation === "horizontal" ? "flex-col items-center" : "flex-row items-center", className)` #### `StepperIndicator` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"span">` - `data-slot="stepper-indicator"`, `data-state={state}` from item context - Renders as a `` circle showing step number or icon slot - State-driven styling using `data-state` Tailwind selectors: - `data-[state=active]`: `bg-primary text-primary-foreground` - `data-[state=completed]`: `bg-primary text-primary-foreground` - `data-[state=pending]`: `border-2 border-muted-foreground/30 text-muted-foreground bg-background` - `data-[state=disabled]`: `border-2 border-muted-foreground/20 text-muted-foreground/50 bg-background` - Base CSS: `"inline-flex size-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-xs font-medium transition-colors"` - Full class: ``` "inline-flex size-8 shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-full text-xs font-medium transition-colors" "data-[state=active]:bg-primary data-[state=active]:text-primary-foreground" "data-[state=completed]:bg-primary data-[state=completed]:text-primary-foreground" "data-[state=pending]:border-2 data-[state=pending]:border-muted-foreground/30 data-[state=pending]:text-muted-foreground data-[state=pending]:bg-background" "data-[state=disabled]:border-2 data-[state=disabled]:border-muted-foreground/20 data-[state=disabled]:text-muted-foreground/50 data-[state=disabled]:bg-background" ``` #### `StepperTitle` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"p">` - `data-slot="stepper-title"`, `data-state={state}` - CSS: `"text-sm font-medium leading-snug data-[state=active]:text-foreground data-[state=pending]:text-muted-foreground data-[state=disabled]:text-muted-foreground/50 data-[state=completed]:text-foreground"` #### `StepperDescription` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"p">` - `data-slot="stepper-description"`, `data-state={state}` - CSS: `"text-xs text-muted-foreground leading-normal data-[state=disabled]:opacity-50"` #### `StepperSeparator` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` - Reads `orientation` from `StepperContext` via `useStepper()` - `data-slot="stepper-separator"`, `data-orientation={orientation}` - In horizontal mode: a horizontal line between items, should grow to fill space — used as a sibling of `StepperItem` in the root flex row. CSS: `"h-px flex-1 bg-border data-[orientation=horizontal]:block data-[orientation=vertical]:hidden"` — but wait, in vertical mode the separator is placed _inside_ `StepperItem` to be a vertical connector. - Correct approach: the separator always renders a line, and its shape adapts: - Horizontal mode (sibling between items): `"h-px min-w-4 flex-1 shrink-0 bg-border"` - Vertical mode (child inside `StepperItem`, below trigger): `"ms-4 mt-1 mb-1 w-px self-start bg-border"` — where `ms-4` aligns it under the center of the 8-unit indicator, and it grows vertically. Actually for the vertical connector, `"ml-[calc(theme(size.4)-0.5px)] mt-0.5 mb-0.5 w-px min-h-4 flex-1 self-stretch bg-border"` aligns it with the indicator center. - Unified approach using `data-orientation`: ``` "shrink-0 bg-border" "data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:flex-1 data-[orientation=horizontal]:min-w-4" "data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px data-[orientation=vertical]:min-h-4 data-[orientation=vertical]:self-stretch data-[orientation=vertical]:ms-[15px]" ``` The `ms-[15px]` (15px = half of 32px indicator) aligns the vertical bar under indicator center. #### `StepperContent` - Props: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` - Reads `value` from `StepperItemContext` and `value` from `StepperContext` - Only renders children when step is active: `if (stepperValue !== itemValue) return null` - `data-slot="stepper-content"`, `data-state={state}` - CSS: `"ms-10 pb-4 text-sm"` — `ms-10` aligns content under the indicator in vertical mode (32px indicator + 8px gap = 40px = ms-10) #### `useStepper` export - Simply re-exports `useCarousel`-style hook: `export { useStepper }` ### Named Exports ``` export { Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperIndicator, StepperTitle, StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent, useStepper, } ``` --- ## 2. File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` This is the **horizontal demo**. It uses `"use client"` and local state to control the active step. ### Pattern ``` "use client" import * as React from "react" import { CheckIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Stepper, StepperContent, StepperDescription, StepperIndicator, StepperItem, StepperSeparator, StepperTitle, StepperTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" export default function StepperDemo() { const [step, setStep] = React.useState("account") const steps = [ { value: "account", label: "Account", description: "Create your account" }, { value: "profile", label: "Profile", description: "Set up your profile" }, { value: "review", label: "Review", description: "Confirm your details" }, ] return (
{steps.map((s, index) => ( x.value === step) > index} > {steps.findIndex(x => x.value === step) > index ? : index + 1} {s.label} {index < steps.length - 1 && } ))}
) } ``` Key decisions: - `"use client"` is required because of `React.useState` - Uses `React.Fragment` with key to interleave items and separators - `completed` prop computed from whether step index precedes current active index - `CheckIcon` from `lucide-react` is used inside `StepperIndicator` for completed steps --- ## 3. File: `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-vertical.tsx` This is the **vertical demo** with content panels and separator inside each item. ### Pattern ``` "use client" import * as React from "react" import { CheckIcon } from "lucide-react" import { Button } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button" import { Stepper, StepperContent, StepperDescription, StepperIndicator, StepperItem, StepperSeparator, StepperTitle, StepperTrigger, } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper" const steps = [ { value: "step-1", title: "Create your account", description: "Enter your email and password", content: "Provide a valid email address and a secure password to get started.", }, { value: "step-2", title: "Verify your email", description: "Check your inbox", content: "We sent a verification link to your email. Click it to continue.", }, { value: "step-3", title: "Complete your profile", description: "Add your details", content: "Fill in your name, bio, and profile picture to personalize your account.", }, ] export default function StepperVertical() { const [step, setStep] = React.useState("step-1") const currentIndex = steps.findIndex(s => s.value === step) function handleNext() { const next = steps[currentIndex + 1] if (next) setStep(next.value) } function handleBack() { const prev = steps[currentIndex - 1] if (prev) setStep(prev.value) } return (
{steps.map((s, index) => ( index} > {currentIndex > index ? : index + 1}
{s.title} {s.description}
{index < steps.length - 1 && }

{s.content}

{currentIndex > 0 && ( )} {currentIndex < steps.length - 1 && ( )}
))}
) } ``` --- ## 4. Registry Entries ### `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts` — Insertion Insert after the closing brace of the `spinner` entry (line 563, `},`) and before the `switch` entry (line 564). The new block to insert is: ```typescript { name: "stepper", type: "registry:ui", dependencies: ["class-variance-authority"], files: [ { path: "ui/stepper.tsx", type: "registry:ui", }, ], }, ``` **Exact insertion point**: After line 563 (` },`) which closes the `spinner` entry, before line 564 (` {` which opens `switch`). The new block sits at lines 564–572, pushing `switch` and everything after down by 9 lines. Note: `class-variance-authority` is listed as a dependency because CVA is used internally for separator/indicator variants. This matches the `spinner` entry which also lists it. Lucide icons used in demos are not listed as a component dependency (they are in the examples, not the UI component itself — the component itself renders children for the indicator slot). ### `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` — Insertion Insert after line 2124 (the `},` closing the `spinner-item` entry) and before line 2125 (`{` opening `switch-demo`). Two new entries: ```typescript { name: "stepper-demo", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-demo.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, { name: "stepper-vertical", type: "registry:example", registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button"], files: [ { path: "examples/stepper-vertical.tsx", type: "registry:example", }, ], }, ``` Note: `stepper-vertical` also lists `button` as a `registryDependency` because the demo uses ` ) } ``` Wait — there is an issue with the inline `React.useContext` calls inside the render function body of `StepperTrigger`. Hooks cannot be called conditionally. Let me re-think: The cleanest approach: `StepperTrigger` calls `useStepper()` and `useStepperItem()` directly. Both are always called unconditionally at the top. The `aria-current` check happens inline as a plain expression using already-stored variables. ```typescript function StepperTrigger({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"button">) { const { value: activeValue, onValueChange, orientation } = useStepper() const { value, disabled } = useStepperItem() return ( ) } ``` #### `StepperIndicator` ```typescript function StepperIndicator({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"span">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = disabled ? "disabled" : activeValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" return ( {children} ) } ``` Note that the `state` derivation is duplicated in both `StepperItem` and in `StepperIndicator` / `StepperTitle` / `StepperDescription`. This is intentional — each sub-component independently reads from both contexts to set its own `data-state`. This is cleaner than trying to pass computed state down through a third context value, and keeps each component self-contained. #### `StepperTitle` ```typescript function StepperTitle({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"p">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = disabled ? "disabled" : activeValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" return (

"disabled" : activeValue === value ? "active" : completed ? "completed" : "pending" return (

) } ``` To reduce the state derivation duplication, a small helper at the module level is the right call: ```typescript function deriveStepState( activeValue: string, itemValue: string, disabled: boolean, completed: boolean ): "active" | "completed" | "pending" | "disabled" { if (disabled) return "disabled" if (activeValue === itemValue) return "active" if (completed) return "completed" return "pending" } ``` #### `StepperSeparator` ```typescript function StepperSeparator({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { const { orientation } = useStepper() return (

) } ``` #### `StepperContent` ```typescript function StepperContent({ className, children, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) { const { value: activeValue } = useStepper() const { value, disabled, completed } = useStepperItem() const state = deriveStepState(activeValue, value, disabled, completed) if (state !== "active") { return null } return (
{children}
) } ``` --- ## 9. Step-by-Step Implementation Sequence 1. **Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`**: - Write contexts (`StepperContext`, `StepperItemContext`) - Write `useStepper` hook - Write `deriveStepState` helper - Write all 8 sub-components in order: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent` - Add named export block at the bottom 2. **Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`**: - Horizontal demo with 3 steps, local state, `CheckIcon` for completed steps 3. **Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-vertical.tsx`**: - Vertical demo with 3 steps, Back/Continue buttons, content panels 4. **Modify `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`**: - Insert 9-line `stepper` entry between the end of the `spinner` entry (after line 563) and the start of the `switch` entry (before what is currently line 564) 5. **Modify `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`**: - Insert `stepper-demo` and `stepper-vertical` entries between the end of the `spinner-item` entry (after line 2124) and the start of the `switch-demo` entry (before what is currently line 2125) --- ## 10. If consumers use a different indicator size, this will be misaligned. This matches the usage pattern specified and is simpler than supporting uncontrolled mode. Consumers must manage state themselves — the demos show this with `React.useState`. **Challenge 4: Separator orientation in vertical layout** `StepperSeparator` reads `orientation` from the root `StepperContext`, not from its positional context (sibling vs. child). In horizontal mode, separators are siblings of `StepperItem`. In vertical mode, they are children of `StepperItem`. The orientation context correctly drives the CSS in both cases. The consumer is responsible for placing the separator in the right structural position. **Challenge 5: `data-state` on `StepperItem` wrapper vs. trigger** Both `StepperItem` and `StepperTrigger` set `data-state`. This is intentional duplication — `StepperItem`'s `data-state` allows parent CSS selectors (`group-data-[state=active]/stepper-item:...`) on children. `StepperTrigger`'s `aria-current` handles the accessibility concern separately. --- ## 11. Verification Steps 1. **TypeScript compilation**: Run `tsc --noEmit` from `apps/v4/` — all components should have proper types, no implicit `any` 2. **Registry validation**: Run `npx shadcn build` or the project's registry build command to verify both `_registry.ts` entries parse correctly 3. **Horizontal layout test**: Render `stepper-demo.tsx` — verify steps are side-by-side, separator fills available width, indicator/title stack vertically within each item 4. **Vertical layout test**: Render `stepper-vertical.tsx` — verify separator is a vertical line aligned under indicator, content panels show/hide correctly 5. **State transitions**: Click each step trigger — verify `data-state` changes on `StepperItem`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle` update styles correctly 6. **Disabled step**: Add `disabled` to one item — verify trigger is `disabled`, styles reflect muted/opacity treatment 7. **Keyboard navigation**: Tab through triggers — verify focus rings appear correctly matching the ring pattern from `button.tsx` 8. **`aria-current="step"`**: Inspect active trigger in browser DevTools — verify attribute is present on active step, absent on others 9. **`aria-hidden`**: Verify `StepperIndicator` and `StepperSeparator` both have `aria-hidden="true"` in rendered output 10. **`useStepper` hook**: Call `useStepper()` outside a `` — verify error is thrown with the correct message --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - Here's the complete implementation plan for the **Stepper** compound component:

## What will be created

**3 new files:**
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` — the component
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx` — horizontal demo
- `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-vertical.tsx` — vertical demo with content panels + Back/Continue buttons

**2 registry entries appended** (no existing entries touched):
- `ui/_registry.ts` — `stepper` entry between `spinner` and `switch`
- `examples/_registry.ts` — `stepper-demo` and `stepper-vertical` entries between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo`

## Architecture

**Two React contexts** (matches `carousel.tsx` pattern):
- `StepperContext` — root state: `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation`
- `StepperItemContext` — per-step state: `value`, `disabled`, `completed`

**8 sub-components** + exported `useStepper` hook:
`Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent`

**Step states** (`data-state`): `active` | `completed` | `pending` | `disabled`  
**No new npm dependencies** — only React + the existing `cn` utility

## Layout behavior
- **Horizontal**: `Stepper` is `flex-row`; `StepperSeparator` is `h-px flex-1` between sibling items
- **Vertical**: `Stepper` is `flex-col`; `StepperSeparator` is `w-px ms-[15px]` inside each item; `StepperContent` shows `ms-10` (aligned to text beside the 32px indicator)

content.text:
Everything is in place. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

## What was created

### 3 new files

**`registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** — The compound component library:
- **`StepperContext`** — root context sharing `value`, `onValueChange`, and `orientation`
- **`StepperItemContext`** — per-step context sharing `value`, `disabled`, `completed`
- **`useStepper()`** — exported hook for consumer access
- **8 sub-components**: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperIndicator`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent`

Key design details:
- Step states (`active` / `completed` / `pending` / `disabled`) are set as `data-state` on `StepperItem` and targeted via `group-data-[state=*]/stepper-item:` Tailwind variants
- Horizontal layout: `flex-row`, separator is `h-px flex-1 mt-4`
- Vertical layout: `flex-col`, separator is `w-px ms-[15px]` (centers the 1px line under the 32px indicator), content panel offset is `ms-10`
- `StepperContent` returns `null` when the step isn't active

**`registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** — Horizontal 3-step wizard with check icons for completed steps and Back/Continue navigation

**`registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-vertical.tsx`** — Vertical wizard with `StepperContent` panels (expandable description + inline navigation) per step

### 2 registry insertions (no existing lines touched)

- **`ui/_registry.ts`** — `stepper` inserted alphabetically between `spinner` and `switch`
- **`examples/_registry.ts`** — `stepper-demo` + `stepper-vertical` inserted between `spinner-item` and `switch-demo`
